title: Non Surgical Weight Loss Options in Malta: Your Complete Guide
excerpt: Melissa had done everything right. She had lost the weight — quietly, steadily, over the course of a year. She had changed what she ate, started walking every evening after the kids were in bed, and logged every meal in an app that made her feel…
body_text: Last reviewed by the Carisma Medical Team — 2026 Melissa had done everything right. She had lost the weight — quietly, steadily, over the course of a year. She had changed what she ate, started walking every evening after the kids were in bed, and logged every meal in an app that made her feel like a scientist studying her own life. And it had worked. The scale told her so. But she stood in the dressing room one afternoon looking at her reflection and felt a familiar frustration. Her body had changed. But not in the way she had imagined. The places she most wanted to see change — the soft roll above her waistband, the inner thighs, the area just below her bra line — seemed untouched by everything she had done. Her weight was lower. Her confidence was not. She started searching for non surgical weight loss options in Malta that might finally address the stubborn pockets that diet and movement alone could not reach. She is not alone. And what she was looking for — targeted, non-invasive body treatment in a clinical setting — is not vanity. It is a completely valid next step. Key Takeaways - Non surgical weight loss options in Malta include fat freezing, fat dissolving injections, EMS muscle stimulation, and lipocavitation - These treatments target localised fat deposits — they complement, not replace, sustained weight management - Results may vary for each individual depending on the area, fat volume, and body response - Combining two or three treatments in a structured programme often produces better outcomes than a single treatment - Every journey at Carisma Slimming starts with a free personalised consultation — no pressure, no guesswork What Non Surgical Weight Loss Actually Means The difference between weight loss and body contouring It is worth being honest about language here, because it matters. Non surgical treatments — fat freezing, fat dissolving injections, EMS muscle stimulation, lipocavitation — are not weight loss treatments in the clinical sense. They will not change the number on your scale in any significant way. What they do is reduce localised subcutaneous fat deposits and improve body shape and contour in specific areas. True weight loss — the reduction of total body fat across all areas — requires a sustained caloric deficit, hormonal balance, and often medical support. That is what a medically supervised, clinically supervised programme like GLP-1 treatment addresses. GLP-1 medications work through appetite suppression and improving insulin resistance, making them a powerful tool for overall metabolic rate management. Body contouring treatments, by contrast, are precise. They target the fat that remains after weight loss, or fat that is biologically resistant to diet and exercise due to where it sits in the body's storage hierarchy. Subcutaneous fat in areas like the lower abdomen, inner thighs, and flanks tends to be among the last to reduce through lifestyle changes alone — and that is not a failure of effort. It is a function of how the body stores and releases fat, which is partly genetic and partly hormonal. Who benefits most from non-surgical approaches Non surgical body slimming treatments in Malta are most effective for people who: Are already at or close to a healthy weight but have specific areas they would like to refine • Have completed a weight loss programme and want to address residual stubborn fat • Are not suitable for or do not want surgical options such as liposuction • Want to add targeted muscle tone alongside fat reduction Results may vary for each individual, and these treatments work best when combined with a broadly healthy lifestyle. They are not a substitute for sustained weight management. However, in the right context, they are a genuinely evidence-supported option worth understanding in full. Fat Freezing (Cryolipolysis) in Malta How it works and what areas it targets Fat freezing — clinically known as cryolipolysis — is one of the most researched non invasive slimming treatments available in Malta and internationally. The science behind it is specific: fat cells (adipocytes) are selectively sensitive to cold temperatures that the overlying skin can tolerate without harm. By delivering precisely controlled cooling to approximately 4°C for a 35–60 minute cycle, the treatment triggers a process called apoptosis — programmed cell death — in the fat cells in the treated area. The dead cells are then gradually cleared by the body's lymphatic system and macrophages over the following 6–12 weeks. The overlying skin, blood vessels, and nerves are unaffected, because they tolerate the cold better than fat cells do. This was first described by researchers Manstein et al. in 2008 and has since been replicated across multiple peer-reviewed studies. Common treatment areas include the lower abdomen, flanks, inner thighs, upper arms, back fat, and under the chin. The applicator is placed against the skin and draws the tissue gently into a vacuum cup — you feel intense cold for the first few minutes, which subsides as the area becomes numb. Expected results and number of sessions A systematic review by Ingargiola et al., published in the Aesthetic Surgery Journal (2015), analysed 19 studies and found a mean reduction in the subcutaneous fat layer of between 14.67% and 28.5% per treated area per session, depending on methodology and body area. Results typically become visible at 4–8 weeks and reach their maximum at around 3 months post-treatment. Many people require two or more sessions per area to achieve their desired outcome. Results may vary for each individual depending on the amount of fat present, the area treated, and how the body responds to the clearance process. You can find out more about what our fat freezing treatment at Carisma Slimming involves, including what each session includes and what realistic outcomes look like for the areas most commonly treated. Fat Dissolving Injections in Malta Fat dissolving injections use a synthetic form of deoxycholic acid — a substance that occurs naturally in the body and helps break down dietary fat — to destroy fat cell membranes in targeted areas. Once the fat cells are disrupted, their contents are processed by the lymphatic system and gradually eliminated. Unlike fat freezing, which uses cold to trigger apoptosis, fat dissolving injections work through a chemical disruption of the cell wall. The result is similar: localised reduction in fat volume in the treated area. The treatment is delivered via fine needles directly into the subcutaneous fat layer. What areas respond best Fat dissolving injections are particularly well-suited to small, localised areas where precise targeted fat reduction is needed. The most commonly treated areas are: Under the chin (submental fat — the so-called "double chin") • Inner thighs • Small localized pockets on the abdomen • Bra-line fat • Inner knees The treatment is less suited to larger, diffuse areas where fat freezing or lipocavitation may offer broader coverage. How many sessions are typical Most clients require between two and four sessions per area, spaced 4–6 weeks apart to allow for adequate clearance between treatments. Swelling and tenderness at the injection site in the days following treatment is normal and expected — it is part of the inflammatory process the treatment relies on. Results are typically visible from 4 weeks after each session, with full effect assessed at 6–8 weeks. Muscle Stimulation (EMS) for Body Contouring Malta How EMS builds muscle and reduces fat simultaneously EMS — electromagnetic muscle stimulation — stands apart from the other non invasive slimming treatments because it does not primarily target fat. It targets muscle. And in doing so, it also produces measurable fat reduction in treated areas. High-intensity focused electromagnetic technology delivers pulsed electromagnetic energy that penetrates through the skin and fat layer to stimulate the underlying muscle fibres. These contractions are supramaximal — they reach an intensity that the voluntary nervous system cannot produce through exercise alone. A standard 30-minute session is estimated to induce approximately 20,000 muscle contractions in the targeted muscle group. The metabolic demands of these extreme contractions also trigger localised lipolysis — the breakdown of fat stored in and around the muscle tissue. A pivotal multicentre study by Kinney et al. (2019, Aesthetic Surgery Journal) found a mean 19.5% reduction in subcutaneous fat and a mean 16.3% increase in muscle thickness in treated abdominal areas at two months' follow-up. This dual effect — building muscle and reducing fat simultaneously — is what makes EMS particularly relevant for those who want visible muscle tone alongside body contouring. Many clients report improved ease of movement and more energy in daily activities after completing a course of treatment. Best candidates for EMS treatment EMS treatment at our Malta clinic is particularly well-suited to: Those who want visible muscle definition in areas like the abdomen or glutes • Women recovering fitness after pregnancy, where muscle tone has been lost • Those who find intensive exercise limited by joint pain or mobility • Those who have completed weight loss and want to refine and tone the underlying musculature Our muscle stimulation treatment is delivered in a comfortable, non-invasive session. No downtime is required — some clients return to their daily routine immediately after. Lipocavitation in Malta How ultrasound breaks down fat cells Lipocavitation uses low-frequency ultrasound waves to target subcutaneous fat deposits. The ultrasound energy creates microscopic pressure waves within the fat tissue, causing the fat cell membranes to rupture through a process called cavitation. The fat released from the disrupted cells — primarily triglycerides — is then processed through the lymphatic system and liver. Lipocavitation tends to work on broader areas than fat dissolving injections, making it suitable for larger surface areas such as the abdomen, thighs, hips, and flanks. It is non-invasive: a handheld probe is moved across the skin surface during the session, and most clients experience only mild warmth and a low humming sensation. What to expect during and after treatment Each lipocavitation session typically lasts 30–60 minutes depending on the area being treated. There is no downtime, though it is generally recommended to drink plenty of water in the 24–48 hours following treatment to support the lymphatic clearance of released fat. Light movement or lymphatic drainage massage after a session can also support the elimination process. A course of 6–10 sessions is typically recommended to achieve visible body contouring, with results building progressively over the course of treatment. As with all body slimming treatments in Malta, results may vary for each individual based on individual factors including fat distribution, age, and skin condition. Complementary Treatments: Skin Tightening and Lymphatic Drainage When to combine treatments for best results Body contouring treatments address fat. But there are two related concerns that often benefit from their own targeted treatment: skin laxity and fluid retention. Skin tightening uses radiofrequency or similar technologies to stimulate collagen production in the dermis, improving the firmness and texture of skin in areas that may have lost elasticity due to weight loss, ageing, or pregnancy. It is commonly paired with fat freezing or lipocavitation to address the skin surface as well as the fat layer beneath. Lymphatic drainage is a gentle massage technique that stimulates the lymphatic system — the body's primary waste clearance network. In the context of body contouring, lymphatic drainage helps the body eliminate the broken-down fat cell debris more efficiently after treatments like fat freezing, fat dissolving injections, or lipocavitation. It also reduces post-treatment swelling and can address puffiness and fluid retention that contributes to a "heavier" appearance. At Carisma Slimming, our clinical team in Malta will advise on which combination of treatments is most appropriate for your individual goals and body type. Combining treatments intelligently produces better outcomes than any single treatment used in isolation — but the right combination depends on your specific starting point and what you are trying to achieve. Which Non Surgical Treatment Is Right for You? Matching treatment to goal and body area There is no single "best" non surgical slimming treatment in Malta — there is only the right treatment for your goal, your body area, and your starting point. Here is a broad guide: | Goal | Most Relevant Treatment | |------|------------------------| | Reduce a specific fat pocket (chin, flank, inner thigh) | Fat freezing or fat dissolving injections | | Improve muscle tone and reduce abdominal fat | EMS muscle stimulation | | Target larger areas like abdomen, thighs, hips | Lipocavitation | | Improve skin firmness after fat reduction | Skin tightening | | Support lymphatic clearance and reduce puffiness | Lymphatic drainage | | Overall body composition change and weight reduction | Medically supervised GLP-1 programme | What many of our clients in Malta find is that the most effective approach combines two or three treatments in a structured programme — for example, lipocavitation followed by lymphatic drainage, alongside a medically supervised nutrition and lifestyle plan. The importance of a consultation before booking No guide — however detailed — can tell you which treatment is right for your body without knowing you. The amount of subcutaneous fat in an area, your skin's condition, your medical history, and your personal goals all influence which treatments will produce the best outcome for you. That is why every journey at Carisma Slimming begins with a comprehensive consultation. We assess your body composition, discuss your history with weight and previous treatments, and recommend a personalised treatment path. We will never recommend treatments for the sake of it. Our clinical team in Malta takes your time, your investment, and your results seriously. Book a free consultation at Carisma Slimming and let us help you understand exactly which non surgical options are right for your body and your goals — without pressure and without guesswork. FAQs About Non Surgical Weight Loss Options in Malta Do non surgical fat loss treatments actually work? Yes — within the right expectations. Treatments like fat freezing, fat dissolving injections, EMS, and lipocavitation all have peer-reviewed clinical evidence supporting their effectiveness for localised fat reduction and body contouring. A systematic review of cryolipolysis found mean fat layer reductions of 14.67–28.5% per treated area per session. EMS studies show both muscle thickness increases and fat reduction. What they do not do is replace sustained weight management — they work best for targeted refinement alongside, or after, a healthy lifestyle programme. Are non surgical body treatments safe? The body slimming treatments offered at medically supervised clinics in Malta are non-invasive, do not require anaesthesia, and carry no surgical risks. Fat freezing can cause temporary redness, numbness, and bruising in the treated area. Fat dissolving injections produce temporary swelling and tenderness. EMS is not suitable for those with pacemakers or metal implants. Lipocavitation is generally very well tolerated. A clinical consultation before any treatment is essential to screen for any contraindications specific to your health history. How many sessions do I need to see results from body contouring? This depends on the treatment, the area, and the individual. Fat freezing typically produces visible results at 4–8 weeks, with optimal results at 3 months; most areas benefit from two or more sessions. Fat dissolving injections typically require 2–4 sessions per area. Lipocavitation courses are usually 6–10 sessions. EMS studies use protocols of 4 sessions over 2 weeks as a baseline. Your Carisma clinical team will advise on the specific course of sessions best suited to your goals. Results may vary for each individual based on factors including starting fat volume and lymphatic function. Can non surgical treatments replace weight loss? No — and any clinic that suggests otherwise is not being honest with you. Non surgical body contouring treatments address localised subcutaneous fat deposits. They do not reduce overall body weight in any meaningful way, and they do not address the hormonal, metabolic, or nutritional factors that drive weight gain. If you have significant weight to lose, a medically supervised weight loss programme is the appropriate starting point. Body contouring treatments are most effective as a complement to weight loss — either during a programme or after reaching a stable, healthy weight. Is fat freezing painful? Most clients describe fat freezing as uncomfortable rather than painful, particularly in the first five to ten minutes of each cycle as the intense cold takes effect. After that initial period, the treated area becomes numb and the sensation resolves. Post-treatment, the area may feel tender, sore, or bruised for a few days to two weeks, similar to the sensation after an intense workout. Some people experience temporary numbness in the treated area for several weeks. The treatment is well-tolerated in clinical settings, and there is no recovery period that prevents normal daily activity immediately afterwards. Which non surgical treatment is best for belly fat in Malta? The honest answer is: it depends on how much belly fat is present and what you are trying to achieve. For larger amounts of subcutaneous abdominal fat, lipocavitation is often a good starting point as it covers broader surface area per session. For more targeted, defined pockets — particularly lower abdominal fat or flanks — fat freezing tends to produce more precise results. EMS is the right choice if you also want to build visible muscle definition alongside fat reduction. A consultation with our Carisma clinical team in Malta will help you identify which treatment, or combination of treatments, suits your specific abdominal area and your goals. Ready to Find the Right Treatment for You? You do not need to have all the answers before you reach out. You do not need to have tried everything else first, or have already lost a certain amount of weight, or be certain about which treatment is right. That is exactly what a consultation is for. At Carisma Slimming Malta, our medically supervised team will take the time to understand your body, your history, and your goals. Then we will recommend a personalised plan that makes sense for you — not a generic package, and not a treatment that is right for someone else's body. Picture yourself eight weeks from now, carrying your shopping without strain, climbing the stairs without thinking about it, wearing something you have been putting off wearing. That life is not out of reach. It is a conversation away. Book your free slimming consultation at Carisma today. Come in, talk to us, and leave with a clear plan. Aesthetic Surgery Journal 2019) with specific quantitative findings. Trustworthiness is high: the post explicitly distinguishes body contouring from weight loss, states contraindications (EMS and pacemakers), repeats 'results may vary' three times, and includes the candid line 'any clinic that suggests otherwise is not being honest with you' — a trust-building admission that reduces over-promise risk.
